req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 4
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three orthogonal concerns: (1) accepted MIME types (PDF, PNG, JPG), (2) maximum file size of 10 MB, and (3) per-user rate limiting. EP partitions valid/invalid MIME types and valid/invalid sizes. BVA covers the 10 MB boundary (exactly 10 MB accepted, 10 MB+1 byte rejected). NEG covers disallowed MIME types (415) and oversized files (413). STC covers the rate-limit state transition from allowed to throttled. DT covers the combination of size and MIME type deciding acceptance. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create/tear-down artefacts and uses an authenticated session provided by the auth_session fixture.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_succeeds
preconditions: A valid PDF file of 1 MB exists
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
POST a 1 MB PDF file to /documents/upload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates virus_scan status is queued
trace: R-WEB-080 — document stored and virus scan queued
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_succeeds(auth_session, make_file):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_mb=1, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued", "virus_scan not queued"

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: A PDF file of 11 MB exists
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
POST an 11 MB PDF file to /documents/upload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing file size
trace: R-WEB-080 — error pathway: 413 when size > 10 MB
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413(auth_session, make_file):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_mb=11, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("large.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "size" in error_text or "large" in error_text or "limit" in error_text

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: A valid .exe file of 1 MB exists
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
POST a 1 MB application/octet-stream file to /documents/upload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing unsupported media type
trace: R-WEB-080 — error pathway: 415 when mime not in allow-list
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(auth_session, make_file):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_mb=1, mime="application/octet-stream", ext="exe")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("malware.exe", file_bytes, "application/octet-stream")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 415, f"Expected 415, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "media" in error_text or "mime" in error_text or "type" in error_text

tests.name: test_upload_file_exactly_10mb_is_accepted
preconditions: A PNG file of exactly 10 MB (10485760 bytes) exists
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
POST a PNG file of exactly 10485760 bytes to /documents/upload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: File is not rejected due to size
trace: R-WEB-080 — BVA upper boundary: exactly 10 MB must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10485760

def test_upload_file_exactly_10mb_is_accepted(auth_session, make_file_bytes):
    file_bytes = make_file_bytes(size_bytes=MAX_BYTES, mime="image/png", ext="png")
    assert len(file_bytes) == MAX_BYTES, "Fixture must produce exactly 10 MB"
    resp = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("boundary.png", file_bytes, "image/png")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Boundary 10 MB rejected: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body

tests.name: test_upload_file_one_byte_over_10mb_is_rejected
preconditions: A JPG file of 10485761 bytes exists
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
POST a JPG file of 10485761 bytes to /documents/upload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: One byte over the boundary is sufficient to trigger rejection
trace: R-WEB-080 — BVA upper boundary + 1: 10 MB + 1 byte must be rejected with 413
tests.executable:
import pytest

OVER_LIMIT_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1  # 10485761

def test_upload_file_one_byte_over_10mb_is_rejected(auth_session, make_file_bytes):
    file_bytes = make_file_bytes(size_bytes=OVER_LIMIT_BYTES, mime="image/jpeg", ext="jpg")
    assert len(file_bytes) == OVER_LIMIT_BYTES, "Fixture must produce exactly limit+1 bytes"
    resp = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("oversize.jpg", file_bytes, "image/jpeg")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413 for limit+1, got: {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_triggers_after_threshold_uploads
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known (read from RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD env var or config)
Rate-limit counter for the user is reset before the test
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
Upload a valid 1 MB PDF exactly N times in rapid succession
Perform one additional upload (N+1)
Capture the HTTP response status of the final request
expected: The (N+1)th upload returns 429 Too Many Requests
Response body or Retry-After header indicates rate limiting
trace: STC: transition from allowed state to throttled state
tests.executable:
import os
import pytest

def test_rate_limit_triggers_after_threshold_uploads(
    auth_session, make_file, reset_rate_limit
):
    threshold = int(os.environ.get("RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD", 5))
    reset_rate_limit(auth_session)  # <fixture/helper>
    file_bytes = make_file(size_mb=1, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    for i in range(threshold):
        r = auth_session.post(
            "/documents/upload",
            files={"file": (f"doc_{i}.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        )
        assert r.status_code in (200, 201), f"Upload {i} failed unexpectedly: {r.status_code}"
    over_limit = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("over_limit.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
    )
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429, f"Expected 429, got: {over_limit.status_code}"

tests.name: test_upload_valid_jpg_and_png_both_accepted
preconditions: A valid JPG file of 1 MB exists
A valid PNG file of 1 MB exists
actions: Authenticate as a valid employee
POST a 1 MB JPG file to /documents/upload; capture response
POST a 1 MB PNG file to /documents/upload; capture response
expected: Both responses indicate virus_scan is queued
trace: DT: MIME type in {image/jpeg, image/png} × size <= 10 MB => accept
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("mime,ext", [
    ("image/jpeg", "jpg"),
    ("image/png",  "png"),
])
def test_upload_valid_jpg_and_png_both_accepted(auth_session, make_file, mime, ext):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_mb=1, mime=mime, ext=ext)
    resp = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": (f"onboarding.{ext}", file_bytes, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"{mime} upload failed: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued"
